commit.message:
Map Wyckoff market phases to ATR regime keys across pipeline

The pipeline's classify_regime() returns Wyckoff phases (DISTRIBUTION,
TREND_UP, etc.) but ATR multiplier lookup, ML inference, and regime
scoring expected simple keys (bullish/neutral/bearish). This caused
DISTRIBUTION regime to default to neutral multipliers instead of bearish.

- helpers.py: Add _wyckoff_map for ATR target multiplier lookup
- runner.py: Add _WYCKOFF_TO_ATR for regime pass-through to RR calc
- ml_20d_inference.py: Recognize TREND_UP/MODERATE_UP as bullish boost,
  DISTRIBUTION as bearish penalty in calibration heuristics
- scoring_config.py: Add DISTRIBUTION (0.80) to REGIME_MULTIPLIERS

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Debounce tournament WebSocket event refetches to prevent API flooding

Rapid-fire game_started/game_ended/player_joined events during active
tournaments were causing every spectator to hammer the API simultaneously.
Now coalesces events within a 2s window into a single fetch, with an
isFetching guard to prevent overlapping requests. tournament_started and
tournament_ended remain immediate since they fire once.

commit.message:
fix(auth): invalidate queries after SIGNED_IN to unblock stalled fetches

When GoTrue refreshes the token internally during page load, it fires
SIGNED_IN instead of INITIAL_SESSION. During this refresh, the client
holds an internal lock that blocks all data queries — queryFn never
executes. After the SIGNED_IN events settle (debounced 100ms),
invalidate all queries to force React Query to retry the blocked fetches.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add VFX polish: trails, sparks, screen shake, gem bob, and impact effects

Orbiter: trailing effect (0.3s, fade out). Projectile: trail (0.2s) +
impact flash on hit. Lightning: beam flicker + spark bursts at chain
targets. Aura: pulsing transparency and scale. Enemy death: 8-12
colored parts + white flash. XP gems: sine bob + green sparkle emitter.
Damage numbers: punch scale (1.3x->1x). Level up: expanding ring +
punch-scaled text + white edge flash. Boss spawn: screen shake + "BOSS
INCOMING" warning. Camera shake system for boss events.

commit.message:
Add custom domain sj-lin.top with CNAME

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: deduplicate CIDs in multi-day review email reports

generate_report() reset cid_counter to 0 on each call, causing duplicate
CIDs across days in the combined review email. Add cid_start parameter
and track a running offset in the review loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: direction confidence z-test redesign + market regime computation

Direction confidence was structurally broken (~20% always) due to
percentile-ranked signals rarely exceeding 60/40 thresholds. Replaced
with z-test on mean shift from neutral using logistic CDF approximation.

Market regime was always null because it was never computed. Added
computation to Phase 3 using vol_cone_percentile + EWMA directional
adjustment, and moved regime enum derivation to _recompute_dimensional_scores().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add Cargo.lock for hx-napi to resolve arrow-arith ambiguity

The NAPI crate is excluded from the workspace and resolves
dependencies independently. Without a shared lockfile, it picks
arrow-arith@52.2.0 with a chrono method ambiguity. Bootstrapping
from the workspace lockfile pins compatible versions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- DeckBinderComparison: replace bulk selection with per-tile add-to-binder/wants
  buttons (hover reveals BookmarkPlus + Heart), explode missing cards into individual
  instances, optimistic tile removal, tcg_low price, foiling/edition/collector info
- DeckListView: colored left-border stripe per pitch row, shorter section labels
  (Red/Yellow/Blue), matching border on section headers
- DeckListPanel (catalog right panel): pitch section labels with counts, colored
  left borders, tcg_low price shown per row, expandable card rows showing individual
  printings with foiling/edition info, click card name to open ViewPrintingsDialog
  for printing swap, improved pitch detection (color + numeric pitch field)
- DeckEditorListView: remove redundant "+ Add" buttons (header text + dashed tile),
  add collapse toggle to equipment/weapons section so maindeck gets more focus
- QuickAddCardDialog: silence noisy console.warn for unresolvable hero classes
- deck editor page: binder selector, optimistic qty updates for sidebar stepper,
  add-to-binder on tile view with ownership dot refresh

commit.message:
Rename to Life Dreams Wealth

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Phase 13: advanced ITL components (3 Krylov solvers, 4 preconditioners, multigrid)

New Krylov solvers: CGS (ported from MTL4), BiCGSTAB(ell) with higher-order
stabilization (ported from MTL4, adapted for single right PC), and MINRES
for symmetric indefinite systems (Paige-Saunders algorithm, from scratch).

New preconditioners: ILUT (ILU with threshold/fill limiting), ILDL
(incomplete LDL^T for symmetric systems), block diagonal (dense LU per
block), and SSOR (symmetric SOR with forward+backward sweep, both generic
and compressed2D specializations).

New multigrid framework: full-weighting restriction, linear interpolation
prolongation, and V-cycle/W-cycle driver with preconditioner interface
for use with Krylov solvers. 70/70 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
[Claude] setup-web-testing-Rcg4L (#4)

* Add GitHub Actions workflows to auto-create and auto-merge Claude PRs

- claude-auto-pr.yml: creates a PR automatically when pushing to claude/* branch
- claude-auto-merge.yml: squash-merges the PR to main immediately on open

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JoTfajaxwAcAdGsP9DXK7i

* Add RWD / mobile-friendly layout

- Replace table-based BirthDataForm with CSS Grid (label above field on mobile)
- Tab nav: flex-wrap so tabs wrap on narrow screens
- Location search: flex layout, touch-friendly 40px+ hit targets
- Inputs/buttons: min-height 36–44px for touch usability
- form-input-wide: fluid width (flex: 1) instead of fixed 180px
- geocode dropdown: full-width, box-shadow for legibility
- Media queries reorganised into 768 / 640 / 480px breakpoints:
  - 640px: form stacks vertically, tabs go full-width, grids single-col
  - 480px: smaller fonts, compact data tables, SI chart hides rashi labels
- transit-two-col, dignity-tables-grid, synastry-persons-grid all stack on mobile

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JoTfajaxwAcAdGsP9DXK7i

* Fix: merge auto-PR and auto-merge into single workflow

GitHub's GITHUB_TOKEN cannot trigger pull_request events on other
workflows, so the separate auto-merge workflow never fired.

Solution: create PR and merge it in the same job, immediately after
creation. Also add contents:write permission required for merging.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JoTfajaxwAcAdGsP9DXK7i

* chore: trigger workflow after enabling Actions write permission

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JoTfajaxwAcAdGsP9DXK7i

* improve chart line visibility: thicker strokes and higher opacity

- Aspect lines: min opacity 0.2→0.5, stroke-width 1→1.5-2, wider dash gap
- Zodiac division lines: stroke-width 0.5→1, color #999→#888
- House cusp lines (non-cardinal): color #aaa→#777
- Planet connector lines: stroke-width 0.5→1, color #ccc→#aaa
- Inner circle: color #ccc→#999

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JoTfajaxwAcAdGsP9DXK7i

commit.message:
Update benchmarks: chat template fix and quantization analysis

Root cause of Q4 failures was missing chat template, not quantization
itself. Custom Q4 (with FP32 embeddings) produces correct results.
Full Q4 (including embeddings) still degrades multi-step reasoning.

commit.message:
feat: Add /@username handle redirect for ActivityPub profile URLs

Mastodon and other AP implementations use the actor `url` field to build
profile links. The user actor advertises `/@username` but no route handled
it, resulting in 404. Add HandleRedirectController to redirect /@username
to /users/:username.

commit.message:
fix: robust JSON extraction from ACP agent output

The ACP executor now handles three systematic failure modes when
extracting structured JSON from agent output:

1. Agent instructions not reaching subprocess — auto-forward the
   agent's own instructions property when caller doesn't pass explicit
   ones, and append the output JSON schema to the prompt.

2. Schema mismatch — agents naturally produce rich objects (dicts)
   where schemas expect flat strings. A coercion layer recursively
   walks parsed JSON and converts non-string values to JSON strings
   for string-typed schema fields.

3. Truncated JSON — agents hitting token limits produce incomplete
   JSON. A repair function closes unclosed strings, arrays, and
   objects before retrying parse.

The extraction pipeline is now: extract → parse (with repair) →
validate (with coercion fallback).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
